senses.definition: to repeatedly mix something using a spoon, fork, etc.

examples.x: Beat the eggs until they are fluffy and light in color.
She had to beat the batter for several minutes to remove all the lumps.
He used a whisk to beat the cream into soft peaks.
To make a smooth sauce, you need to beat the ingredients well.
The recipe instructs to beat the butter and sugar until creamy.

senses.definition: to combine different substances together

examples.x: The chef blended various spices to create a flavorful sauce.
The artist skillfully blended colors on the canvas for a harmonious effect.
To achieve the perfect aroma, the perfumer blended different scents.
The bartender blended ingredients to craft a delicious cocktail.

senses.definition: to blend or process food quickly and thoroughly in a blender, food processor, or similar appliance until smooth or finely chopped

examples.x: She decided to blitz the vegetables for a smoother soup.
I’ll blitz the garlic and herbs before adding them to the dish.
We need to blitz the tomatoes for the salsa recipe.
He blitzed the spinach and kale for a healthy green drink.
To save time, she blitzed the ingredients for the dip in one go.

senses.definition: to remove the bones from meat, fish, or poultry, often to make it easier to cook or eat
to kill and prepare animals, typically for food

examples.x: In the traditional practice, the community would butcher a cow for a festive feast.
The family gathers each year to butcher a pig for their winter food supply.
At the slaughterhouse, workers butcher livestock to supply meat to supermarkets.
The family together butchered the chickens they raised for their dinner.

senses.definition: to spread a smooth, creamy substance on something, usually using a knife

examples.x: The chef suggests buttering the bread before grilling it for a crispy texture.
Butter the corn on the cob before sprinkling it with salt.
He enjoys buttering his biscuits before dipping them in gravy.
Don't forget to butter the baking dish before adding the cake batter.

senses.definition: to cut a piece of cooked meat into smaller pieces

examples.x: At the family dinner table, the father skillfully carved the roast turkey.
The chef carefully carved the prime rib roast into generous portions.
With precision and expertise, the caterer carved the glazed ham into bite-sized chunks.
As the holiday feast began, the host carved the roasted chicken.
The barbecue enthusiast proudly carved the smoked brisket into thick slices.

senses.definition: a technique in which herbs or leafy vegetables are finely sliced or shredded into thin, ribbon-like strips
to break a small piece off something

examples.x: The woodworker needed to chip small sections from the block to achieve the desired shape.
When removing tiles, it's essential to chip the grout to avoid damaging the underlying surface.
He chipped a tooth while biting down on a hard piece of candy.

senses.definition: to stir cream very hard until it transforms into butter

examples.x: Before modern appliances, people used to churn cream by hand.
She churned the cream tirelessly until it thickened into butter.
Churning cream requires patience and strong arm muscles.
In pioneer days, families would take turns churning cream for the week.

senses.definition: to trim or cut off excess fat, gristle, or other undesirable portions from meat or poultry before cooking

examples.x: The butcher cleans the ribs by trimming away excess fat.
She cleaned the pork loin before seasoning it with herbs.
He is cleaning the chicken thighs, removing any unwanted skin.
They clean the duck breasts meticulously before searing them.
While preparing dinner, we cleaned the fish fillets to ensure they were ready for grilling.

senses.definition: to remove the central part from a fruit, vegetable, or similar item

examples.x: He carefully cored the pineapple before serving it as a tropical dessert.
She cored the apple and sliced it into pieces for the pie.
The chef demonstrated how to core the bell peppers for stuffing with rice and vegetables.
It's easier to core a pineapple with a specialized tool.
She cored the apples before baking the pie.

senses.definition: to prepare vegetables or fruits, especially tomatoes, by removing the seeds, peeling, and cutting them into big pieces

examples.x: The chef concassed the tomatoes for the sauce, ensuring they were roughly chopped to add texture.
She concasses the fruits meticulously, removing seeds and skins before dicing them into large chunks for the salad.
To prepare the salsa, first, you need to concasse the tomatoes.

senses.definition: to make a substance smooth by beating or mixing

examples.x: Cream the butter and sugar together until light and fluffy.
She creamed the ingredients for the cake batter until smooth.
Use a mixer to cream the eggs and milk for the custard.
Creaming the ingredients evenly distributes flavors throughout the dish.
Cream the cheese and herbs together for a flavorful spread.

senses.definition: to cut something into small, equal, square-shaped pieces

examples.x: She cubes the potatoes for the stew, ensuring they cook evenly.
He cubed the chicken for the curry, making it easier to cook.
They will cube the tofu for the stir-fry tomorrow, adding protein to the dish.
The chef is cubing the carrots for the soup right now, maintaining consistency in size.
While cooking, he was cubing the onions for the salsa, enhancing its flavor.

senses.definition: to flavor food with a mix of spices commonly used in Indian cuisines

examples.x: She curries the chicken with a blend of aromatic spices for a flavorful dish.
He enjoys currying lentils with cumin, coriander, and turmeric for a hearty stew.
Curry the rice with cinnamon, cardamom, and cloves for a fragrant side dish.
Currying the lamb with ginger, garlic, and chili powder adds depth to the dish.
We decided to curry the chickpeas with curry powder and tomatoes for a spicy curry.

senses.definition: to divide a thing into smaller pieces using a sharp object

examples.x: I cut the paper with scissors to make a snowflake.
I need to cut the grass in the backyard with a lawnmower.
Sarah cuts fabric to make a dress.
She cuts the fruit into bite-sized pieces for a fruit salad.
They cut the cake into slices to share with everyone.

senses.definition: to remove seeds from fruits, vegetables, or other foods
to cut food into small cubes

examples.x: She diced the tomatoes before adding them to the salad.
Before cooking, she always dices the chicken into small pieces.
He carefully diced the carrots to ensure even cooking.
They diced the potatoes and fried them for breakfast.
The recipe called for her to dice the apples for the pie filling.

senses.definition: a flattened piece of dough used as a base for various baked goods or pastries
to coat or cover food, typically with flour or breadcrumbs, before cooking

examples.x: The chef instructed the kitchen staff to dredge the chicken breasts in seasoned flour before frying.
When preparing calamari, the chef would dredge the rings in a light tempura batter for a delicate crunch.
Before placing the cutlets in the oven, the cook dredged them in a coating of Parmesan and breadcrumbs.
The catfish was traditionally dredged in a spicy seasoning mix before being pan-fried for a delightful kick.
In the southern-style cooking, they often dredge okra in cornmeal before being fried to perfection.

senses.definition: to add seasonings, sauces, or other toppings to enhance the flavor or appearance of food

examples.x: The chef dressed the salad with a light vinaigrette made from olive oil, balsamic vinegar, and Dijon mustard.
After grilling the steak, he dressed it with a knob of herb butter to add richness and flavor.
She dressed the pasta with freshly grated Parmesan cheese and a sprinkle of chopped parsley.
The chef dressed the sushi rolls with spicy mayo and sesame seeds for added flavor and texture.
The roasted vegetables were dressed with a drizzle of balsamic glaze before serving.

senses.definition: to pour a thin, fine stream of liquid, such as sauce, oil, or syrup, over food

examples.x: To enhance the presentation, the pastry chef drizzled chocolate sauce in a delicate pattern over the dessert.
The home cook decided to drizzle honey over the fresh berries to sweeten the fruit salad.
The chef carefully drizzles olive oil over the bruschetta, giving it a glossy finish.
To create a visually appealing dish, the culinary artist drizzled raspberry coulis over the creamy panna cotta.
The chef artfully drizzled balsamic glaze over the Caprese salad, adding a touch of elegance.

senses.definition: a mixture of beaten eggs and liquid used to brush onto the surface of dough or other baked goods before baking
to stuff one food item inside another
to prepare or cut a piece of meat or fish into boneless, flat pieces, typically removing bones in the process
to break or separate into small, thin pieces, usually using a fork or fingers
to gently mix one ingredient into another by lifting and turning the mixture with a spatula or spoon
to slice food, typically vegetables, into long, thin strips or ribbons

examples.x: She frenches the carrots for the salad, creating elegant ribbons.
Yesterday, he frenched the zucchini for the stir-fry, adding a touch of sophistication to the dish.
Tomorrow, they will french the cucumbers for the appetizer, enhancing its visual appeal.
The chef is frenching the bell peppers for the dish, ensuring uniformity in presentation.
While I was preparing dinner, she was frenching the green beans, adding a gourmet touch to the meal.

senses.definition: to make food look more delicious by decorating it

examples.x: She garnished the salad with cherry tomatoes and fresh herbs.

The dessert was garnished with a dusting of powdered sugar and a mint leaf.

senses.definition: to cut food into small pieces or shreds using a tool with sharp holes

examples.x: Grate the cheese for the pizza using a cheese grater.
She grated carrots to add to the salad for extra crunch.
Grate some lemon zest to add a refreshing touch to the dish.
He carefully grated chocolate to sprinkle on top of the dessert.

senses.definition: to remove the internal organs or intestines, typically from an animal, often for food preparation

examples.x: She guts the fish before grilling it over the fire to ensure it cooks evenly.
They will gut the chickens for the barbecue party tomorrow.

senses.definition: to remove the outer covering or husk from a seed or grain

examples.x: He carefully hulled the almonds to use in his homemade granola.
She asked her brother to hull the sunflower seeds for her snack.
We hired workers to hull the soybeans on the farm before packaging them for sale.

senses.definition: to prepare meat by cutting or separating it at the natural points of articulation, often in preparation for cooking

examples.x: She joints the chicken before roasting it in the oven for dinner.
He jointed the lamb for the barbecue party.
They will joint the turkey for Thanksgiving dinner.
The butcher is jointing the beef for the steak cuts.
While she was cooking, he was jointing the fish for grilling, ensuring it cooked evenly.

senses.definition: to extract liquid from fruits, vegetables, or other sources, typically by pressing or squeezing

examples.x: She juices fresh vegetables every morning for a nutritious start to her day.
We juiced the cucumbers and mint leaves to create a cool and refreshing detox drink.

senses.definition: to cut food, especially vegetables, into thin, matchstick-sized strips

examples.x: She julienne the carrots and bell peppers to add color and texture to the stir-fry.
He will julienne the zucchini and yellow squash for the vegetable medley.
They are julienning the cucumbers for a refreshing crunch in the salad.

senses.definition: to form and press dough or wet clay with the hands

examples.x: The baker had to knead the bread dough to develop its elasticity.
To make homemade pizza, she had to knead the pizza dough until it became smooth.
The potter skillfully kneaded the wet clay on the wheel to shape it into a vase.
The chef demonstrated how to knead the pasta dough for a perfect texture.

senses.definition: to serve or transfer a liquid or food using a ladle

examples.x: She ladles the sauce onto the pasta, ensuring each strand is coated evenly.

He ladled the hot chocolate into mugs, garnishing each with a dollop of whipped cream.
During the feast, guests take turns ladling gravy onto their plates, savoring the rich flavor with each bite of roast turkey.

senses.definition: to crush food into a soft mass

examples.x: She mashed the boiled potatoes with a fork to make creamy mashed potatoes for dinner.

senses.definition: to grind or crush something, especially grains or other materials, using a mechanical device or equipment

examples.x: The farmer used a specialized machine to mill the wheat into fine flour.
In the bakery, they milled the grains to create the base for the bread.
To make homemade pasta, she milled the wheat into fine flour.

senses.definition: to cut meat or other food into very small pieces, usually using a meat grinder or a sharp knife

examples.x: Mince the garlic cloves finely before adding them to the sauce.

Mincing the ginger releases its flavor in the stir-fry.
To make homemade sausage, you need to mince the pork.

senses.definition: to combine two or more distinct substances or elements to form a unified whole

examples.x: The chef carefully mixed the ingredients to create a flavorful sauce.
In the laboratory, scientists routinely mix chemicals to observe their reactions.
She skillfully mixed the colors on the palette to achieve the desired shade for her painting.
Bartenders expertly mix various spirits and juices to craft unique cocktails.
The baker diligently mixed the batter to ensure a smooth and uniform texture for the cake.

senses.definition: to peel or strip away the outer layer of a fruit, vegetable, or other foods

examples.x: She pares the apples before adding them to the pie filling.

senses.definition: to remove the skin or outer layer of something, such as fruit, etc.

examples.x: Peel the banana before eating it.

senses.definition: to sprinkle or season food with ground pepper or peppercorns to add flavor and spice

examples.x: I love to pepper my popcorn with a sprinkle of chili powder for a spicy snack.

senses.definition: to create decorative patterns or designs by forcing a semi-solid mixture, such as whipped cream or dough, through a nozzle or pastry bag with a specific shape

examples.x: She piped beautiful flowers onto the cake with whipped cream.

senses.definition: to remove the pit or stone from a fruit

senses.definition: to pull out the feathers of a dead bird in order to prepare it for cooking

examples.x: She learned from her grandmother how to pluck a duck, a skill passed down through generations.

senses.definition: to remove peas or beans from their outer covering, typically as part of food preparation

senses.definition: to cook food for eating

examples.x: She regularly prepares a healthy breakfast for her family.

Yesterday, he enthusiastically prepared a homemade pizza for dinner.
Right now, the cook is preparing a savory soup in the kitchen.
Why are you always preparing snacks when guests are expected?

senses.definition: to hastily create a meal, typically using whatever ingredients are available

senses.definition: to add salt to food or another substance in order to enhance its flavor

senses.definition: to adjust or modify something according to a specific rate, standard, or size

senses.definition: to mix an egg yolk with its egg whites and then cook it, usually with milk or butter

senses.definition: to remove a thin layer or small amount of something from a surface using a sharp or rough edge

senses.definition: to add spices or salt to food to make it taste better

senses.definition: to remove the seeds from a fruit or vegetable

senses.definition: to cut thin slices or layers from the surface of something

senses.definition: to remove the outer covering from something, often to access what is inside

senses.definition: to cut something into very small pieces

senses.definition: to remove the outer covering or shell from corn, oysters, or other similar foods
to pass a powdered substance through a sieve or fine mesh to remove lumps or impurities

senses.definition: to remove a substance, such as foam or fat,  from the surface of a liquid or object, typically by gently scooping or brushing it off
to remove the outer layer or covering from something
to add spices or flavorful ingredients to a dish to give it more flavor

examples.x: He spiced up the marinade by adding fresh herbs and aromatic spices.

senses.definition: to put a layer of a smooth or soft substance over a surface

senses.definition: to move a spoon, etc. around in a liquid or other substance to completely mix it

examples.x: She gently stirred the soup, ensuring all the flavors blended perfectly before serving.

senses.definition: to remove the seeds from fruits, such as cherries or peaches

examples.x: She stones the cherries before making the pie, ensuring a smooth texture without seeds.

senses.definition: to fill meat or vegetables with a mixture of different ingredients

senses.definition: to make meat softer and easier to eat or cut by breaking down the fibers, typically through pounding or marinating it
to remove the tops and tails or ends of something, such as trimming the stems and leaves from vegetables

examples.x: She trusses the chicken before roasting it in the oven.

senses.definition: to mix ingredients with a wire whisk or fork in cooking or baking to achieve a specific texture

senses.definition: to make food very quickly

senses.definition: to beat or mix rapidly, typically with a utensil such as a whisk

examples.x: She whisked the eggs vigorously to create a fluffy omelette.
